audit_text: `bearings_dir/` package entirely absent. Arch §1.1.6 specifies 5 modules: contract.py (Pydantic models for manifest/state/pending.toml schemas), io.py (atomic TOML read/write via tempfile + os.rename), lifecycle.py (note_directory_context_start(), history.jsonl append + cap), onboarding.py (7-step onboarding ritual, brief composition, bearings__dir_init tool body), pending.py (pending-ops logic, backing for the web route). Currently web/routes/pending.py reads/writes pending.toml directly — no agent-layer separation.
verdict_evidence: Glob `src/bearings/bearings_dir/**/*` returns no files — the package directory is entirely absent on the v1-rebuild branch. `web/routes/pending.py:32-71` confirms direct `tomllib.load` / `tomli_w.dump` calls against `.bearings/pending.toml` with no agent-layer separation; `_save_ops` at lines 62-71 opens the file with `path.open('wb')` and writes directly with `tomli_w.dump` — not the POSIX-atomic tempfile-and-rename the architecture mandates. `git log --first-parent v1-rebuild -- src/bearings/bearings_dir/` shows no commits on the v1-rebuild branch (the four matching commits are from the v0.17.x orphan-history side and never landed on v1-rebuild). No history.jsonl reader exists anywhere in src/bearings (`Grep history.jsonl` returns zero matches).
acceptance_criteria: `src/bearings/bearings_dir/` exists as a Python package with the 5 modules named in arch §1.1.6: contract.py, io.py, lifecycle.py, onboarding.py, pending.py
contract.py exports Pydantic models for manifest.toml / state.toml / pending.toml plus version-schema-version Final[...] constants and validation helpers
io.py performs atomic TOML reads and writes via tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile + os.replace() (POSIX-atomic) — direct `path.open('wb')` writes are forbidden
lifecycle.py exports `note_directory_context_start(...)` and a history.jsonl append-and-cap helper that trims from the head via atomic rename when the cap is exceeded
onboarding.py contains the 7-step onboarding ritual, brief composition, and the bearings__dir_init tool body (the same body wired into bearings_mcp.py per finding 003)
pending.py owns the pending-ops backing logic; `web/routes/pending.py` is reduced to a thin handler that delegates to `bearings_dir.pending` and uses `bearings_dir.io` for atomic writes
`bearings.bearings_dir.*` does not import `bearings.agent.*` per the existing layering rule documented at docs/architecture-v1.md:549
Each module ships a corresponding `tests/test_bearings_dir_<module>.py` with coverage of the happy path plus the atomicity / cap / version-mismatch edge cases
executor_brief: Build the `src/bearings/bearings_dir/` package per architecture §1.1.6. The package was never ported to v1-rebuild — `web/routes/pending.py` currently does its own non-atomic TOML I/O, and there is no `history.jsonl` reader anywhere. Five modules to write (contract / io / lifecycle / onboarding / pending), each with its own test file. Coordinate with finding-003 (bearings_mcp.py): the `bearings__dir_init` tool body lives in `bearings_dir/onboarding.py` and is exposed via the MCP server.

audit_text: `cli/gc.py` missing. Arch §1.1.1 explicitly places `bearings gc uploads` here. v0.17.x had top-level `uploads_gc.py`; arch moved it here. Currently just a stub comment in `cli/app.py`, never registered as a subparser. Needs mark-and-sweep: collect sha256s from DB → walk on-disk shards → delete orphans; reverse walk for DB rows with missing files.
verdict_evidence: Glob `src/bearings/cli/*.py` returns app.py, serve.py, todo.py, _todo_io.py, __init__.py — no gc.py. `cli/app.py:19-22` docstring states verbatim: 'Stubs for serve / init / window / send / here / pending / gc are deferred per arch §1.1.1 + behavior doc; each subsequent item adds its module under cli/ and registers its subparser through build_subparser.' `cli/app.py:75-76` only calls `todo_cli.build_subparser(sub)` and `serve_cli.build_subparser(sub)`. The bare-`bearings` bootstrap message at lines 45-48 still prints 'gc land in subsequent items'. No top-level `uploads_gc.py` exists either (`Glob src/bearings/**/uploads_gc*` returns nothing).
acceptance_criteria: `src/bearings/cli/gc.py` exists and exports `build_subparser(sub)` plus a domain entrypoint conforming to the handlers-stay-thin rule (every subcommand body is a single call into agent/db helpers per arch §1.1.1)

`bearings gc uploads [--retention-days N] [--dry-run]` matches the stdout / stderr / exit-code contract documented at docs/behavior/bearings-cli.md:136-156 (header, per-subdir line, footer, no-match case, retention-days validation)
`cli/app.py` registers the gc subparser via `gc_cli.build_subparser(sub)` and the bare-`bearings` bootstrap notice no longer lists `gc` as deferred

GC implements two-direction sweep: forward (DB sha256s → walk shards → delete orphans on disk) AND reverse (DB rows whose files are missing); age-based TTL via `--retention-days` is honoured for both passes
Negative `--retention-days` exits 2 with the exact stderr message from behavior doc line 156
`tests/test_cli_gc.py` exercises: orphan-on-disk pruned, DB-row-missing-file flagged, dry-run leaves disk untouched, negative retention exits 2, no-match path prints the documented footer
executor_brief: Add `src/bearings/cli/gc.py` and wire it into `cli/app.py`. The behavior contract is fully specified at docs/behavior/bearings-cli.md:136-156 — match it exactly (stdout shapes, exit codes, error messages). Implementation is mark-and-sweep against the content-addressed upload store in `~/.local/share/bearings-v1/uploads/` plus a reverse pass on the `uploads` table. Use `agent/uploads.py` helpers for sha256 + shard-path resolution; do not duplicate that logic in cli/.

audit_text: `bearings_mcp.py` incomplete. Only has `close_session`. The live v0.17.x MCP (what agents use right now) has `bearings__bash`, `bearings__dir_init`, `bearings__get_tool_output`. None carried over to v1. `dir_init` body belongs in `bearings_dir/onboarding.py` per arch. `get_tool_output` is the large-output retrieval escape hatch. The v1 MCP surface advertised to agent sessions is fiction in three places.
verdict_evidence: `agent/bearings_mcp.py` exposes only `close_session`. Line 184-186 docstring acknowledges: 'Today the server exposes only close_session; future tools register the same way (factory function, append to the tools list).' Line 191-193: `tools=[make_close_session_tool(deps)]` — single-element list. `Grep -r 'bearings__bash|bearings__dir_init|bearings__get_tool_output|get_tool_output|dir_init' src/bearings` returns zero matches; none of the three v0.17.x agent-facing tools have been ported. The audit's framing — 'fiction in three places' — is accurate: agent sessions invoking these tool handles will hit MCP unknown-tool errors.
acceptance_criteria: `bearings__bash` tool registered on the bearings MCP server: streams stdout/stderr line-by-line to the agent's terminal pane, returns combined output + exit code, honours `agent/shell.py`'s argv allowlist + bounded timeout + kill-on-timeout (no shell=True)

`bearings__dir_init` tool registered: tool body lives in `bearings_dir/onboarding.py` per arch §1.1.6 line 288 and is wired through bearings_mcp.py only as a dispatch shim — bearings_mcp.py does not duplicate the onboarding ritual

`bearings__get_tool_output` tool registered: takes a `tool_use_id`, returns the captured raw output for that prior tool call (the large-output retrieval escape hatch); cap and storage layout follow `DEFAULT_TOOL_OUTPUT_CAP_CHARS` in `config/constants.py`
`build_bearings_mcp_server(deps)` constructs the server with all four tools (close_session + bash + dir_init + get_tool_output) registered in the `tools=[...]` list
Each tool name is sourced from `bearings.config.constants` Final[str] entries (no inline string literals downstream — same pattern already used for `CLOSE_SESSION_TOOL_NAME`)
Each tool ships a dedicated test file exercising the success path plus at least one error path (timeout, missing tool_use_id, dir already initialised)
executor_brief: Add three tools to the bearings MCP server: `bash`, `dir_init`, `get_tool_output`. Pattern after the existing `close_session` tool — factory function returning `SdkMcpTool[Any]`, registered in `build_bearings_mcp_server`'s tools list, names sourced from `config/constants.py`. The `dir_init` body belongs in `bearings_dir/onboarding.py` (coordinate with finding-001) so bearings_mcp.py just imports + dispatches. `bash` reuses `agent/shell.py`'s argv allowlist and bounded-timeout machinery — do not introduce a parallel implementation.

audit_text: `web/routes/history.py` is a DB full-text search route (`GET /api/history/search?q=`). Arch says `history.py` should be the `history.jsonl` READER for the `.bearings/` directory context. Wrong module, wrong implementation. The search function belongs in `search.py` or `sessions.py`; `history.py` is taken by the wrong thing. Module-namespace collision with #5 sidebar search — the rename has to land jointly.
verdict_evidence: `web/routes/history.py:1-188` is a DB full-text search route: docstring at lines 1-41 advertises `GET /api/history/search?q=<term>`, lines 100-167 implement two-surface LIKE search across the `sessions` and `messages` tables, and the handler at lines 170-184 is decorated `@router.get('/api/history/search', ...)`. There is no `history.jsonl` reader: `Grep history.jsonl src/bearings` returns zero matches. Architecture §1.1.5 (line 252) specifies `web/routes/history.py` as the `history.jsonl` reader. The current implementation occupies the namespace slot the spec assigns to a different responsibility.
acceptance_criteria: The DB full-text search implementation currently in `web/routes/history.py` moves verbatim to `web/routes/search.py` (or merges into `web/routes/sessions.py` per the audit's 'search.py or sessions.py' phrasing); the `GET /api/history/search` URL is preserved if behavior parity is required, otherwise the new path is documented in CHANGELOG
`web/routes/history.py` is rewritten as a `history.jsonl` reader for the `.bearings/` directory context per arch §1.1.5, delegating to `bearings_dir/lifecycle.py` (per finding-001)
Pydantic models follow the rename: `HistorySearchResult` moves to `web/models/search.py`; `web/models/history.py` exposes the new jsonl-entry shape

`web/app.py` includes both routers under their new module paths; the OpenAPI export at `docs/openapi.json` is regenerated bit-for-bit and committed in the same commit (per CLAUDE.md OpenAPI export protocol)
Tests split: `tests/test_search_route.py` covers the moved DB search; `tests/test_history_jsonl_reader.py` covers the new jsonl reader (read, cap, missing-file degrade)
Coordinate with feature-5 sidebar-search finding — the rename lands jointly so the sidebar consumes the new endpoint path in the same commit
executor_brief: Rename: the existing `web/routes/history.py` (DB full-text search) becomes `web/routes/search.py`; a new `web/routes/history.py` is written as the `history.jsonl` reader the architecture requires. This is a module-namespace collision with feature-5 sidebar search per the audit — coordinate landing so the sidebar's consumer path moves in the same commit. The new history.py reader delegates to `bearings_dir/lifecycle.py` (depends on finding-001 landing first or co-landing). Regenerate `docs/openapi.json` per CLAUDE.md.
